turns.user_message: I’ve had this heavy, hollow ache sitting right in the center of my chest for weeks now, and it just refuses to fade. It feels like I’m carrying around a backpack full of wet sand everywhere I go. I try to shake it off and pretend everything is fine, but the weight just pulls me down a little more each day. I look at my hands and wonder why they feel so useless when I try to handle even the simplest routines. It’s exhausting to wake up and immediately feel like I’m already failing at just existing.

That same heavy pressure in my ribs has started to feel completely permanent, like it’s woven into my actual bones. I catch myself flinching whenever I make a tiny mistake, because my body instantly reacts like I’ve committed some massive crime. I keep replaying every little misstep in my head until the ache spreads down my arms and makes my fingers tremble. It’s like my own nervous system is constantly reminding me that I just don’t measure up to anyone else. I try to push through it, but the exhaustion just keeps piling up until I can barely move.

The crushing weight inside my chest has completely taken over, making it hard to even take a full breath without feeling like I’m suffocating on my own inadequacy. Every single thought I have gets filtered through this thick, exhausting fog that tells me I’m fundamentally broken and completely worthless. I can feel my shoulders curling inward, trying to disappear into myself because I’m so convinced I’m just taking up space. It’s a constant, grinding pressure that leaves me completely drained before I even attempt to do anything at all. I just feel like a hollow shell pretending to be a person, and I don’t know how much more of this heavy silence I can carry.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This has been going on for a while.
